one_liner: GOOG bounces +5% to $351 on Dow Jones inclusion day — the June 26 selling climax (82M shares, #1 in 90-day dataset) may be the SC, and today's AR is on schedule; watch for volume drying up in next 2 sessions.

price_action.vol_rank: Today's data not yet in prices API — analysis based on June 26 last close
price_action.intraday_story: The June 26 session (last available technical data) was the selling climax: 82.45M shares (3.29x avg, highest in 90-day dataset), close at $334.69 near the session low. Today's Dow addition is forcing index-fund buying, generating the +4.96% Automatic Rally (AR) that Wyckoff methodology predicts immediately after a Selling Climax. The watchlist price of $351.28 represents a $16.59 AR off the SC low — roughly 5% — which is typical AR magnitude after a climactic event.

news_catalyst.headline: Alphabet Officially Joins the Dow Jones Industrial Average Today, Replacing Verizon
news_catalyst.summary: Effective today, Alphabet replaces Verizon in the 30-stock DJIA — the first reshuffle since Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams joined in late 2024. S&P Global cited AI, cloud infrastructure, and advertising exposure. DJIA-tracking index funds are required to buy GOOG today, providing mechanical buying pressure right at the selling climax support zone. Secondary news: Google limited Meta's Gemini API access due to compute constraints (a demand signal, not weakness), while Australia's YouTube fine proposals and AI talent departures (Jumper, Shazeer) remain headwinds. Earnings July 23 is the next binary event.

technical.summary:
June 26 analysis: GOOG's RSI reached 27.95 — extreme oversold. The 82.45M share selling climax is the largest volume session in the 90-day dataset, 71% above the next biggest spike. The stock has fallen 13% from the May 27 close of $384.60. Close is below the lower Bollinger Band ($336.56), below both EMA50 ($369.34) and EMA200 ($379.82). Today's +5% AR is the Wyckoff-predicted response to the SC.

The key technical question from here: does the AR stall around $350–$360 and then pull back on light volume for the Secondary Test? The BB middle ($358.64) is the first meaningful resistance — a secondary test that holds $333–$336 on below-average volume would be the LPS confirmation for an accumulate signal.

wyckoff_narrative:
June 26's 82.45M share selling climax is the candidate SC event. Today's +4.96% AR is the Automatic Rally following the SC — this is Phase A of Wyckoff accumulation (Stopping the Downtrend) playing out in real time. The sequence now expected: (1) AR to $350–$360, (2) Secondary Test (ST) pulling back to $333–$342 on light volume, (3) if the ST holds, Phase B base-building begins.

The critical confirmation needed: if today's AR volume comes in below the 82.45M SC volume (which it almost certainly will given mechanical index-fund buying vs. retail panic selling on the 26th), the AR-SC volume relationship is correct. Two-three sessions of below-average volume while testing the $336–$342 zone would build the accumulation thesis significantly. Breaking below $333.69 (the SC low) on any volume cancels the pattern.

valuation.summary: DCF base case at $411.13 using normalized FCF ($68.9B 4-year avg, not distorted by $146.4B capex). At today's $351.28, the 17% upside to base case represents a genuine margin of safety for a Grade A business. Earnings July 23 is the next catalyst that will either validate or challenge the DCF inputs.

key_strengths: 82% YoY earnings growth, 37.9% net margins, 38.9% ROE
Analyst consensus 1.43 (Strong Buy), $426 mean target = 21% upside
key_concerns: AI talent departures (Jumper, Shazeer) — Gemini competitive risk
OBV deeply negative (-246M) — distribution has been relentless since mid-June
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals — perfect growth and profitability scores — firmly support the technical bottoming thesis. This is not a business in distress; it's an A-grade business experiencing a sentiment-driven 13% selloff that has created the best entry opportunity in months.

risks: OBV at -246M — institutional selling has been sustained and heavy; AR may be index-mechanical, not organic
Earnings July 23: capex guidance and Gemini growth vs. Claude/GPT comparisons will determine whether the bottom holds
AI talent risk accelerating — if more key researchers depart, the Gemini competitive story deteriorates
Break below $333.69 SC low on volume invalidates the bottoming pattern and opens path to $310 stop
